classObjective: Interpret intercepts in the context of real-world situations modeled by linear functions and rewrite linear equations in equivalent forms.
workedExamples: 4-1, Example 2 — Graph Using x- and y-Intercepts (continued)
warmUp: What does the y-intercept represent in a real-world context of earning money?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: initial value interpretation (y-intercept); predicting when a quantity reaches zero (x-intercept); rewriting linear equations in different forms
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice interpreting intercepts in context and rewriting linear equations in equivalent forms
independentPractice: Practice problems interpreting intercepts in real-world linear models and rewriting equations
assessment: Exit ticket: interpret the intercepts of a linear model for a savings account context
capReflection: How does understanding intercepts in context help you make predictions about real-world situations?
